# Restacked - Context Index

## Start Here
- INTENT-BUILD-RESTACKED.md - Read this first. Always. Full project context in one file.

## Core Context
- STACK.md - Build tools, MCP server, GitHub integration, deployment targets, what is not in scope.
- ARCHITECTURE.md - Three-layer model, session flow, bootstrap flow, self-improvement loop, file ownership.
- DOMAIN.md - Terminology, business rules, user types, non-negotiables.
- UX-PATTERNS.md - "Make it so" trigger, all user flows, onboarding site, error states, anti-patterns.
- AUTH.md - PAT prototype, OAuth launch plan, GitHub identity model, permission model.
- COMPETITORS.md - What exists, what each tool does well, what to avoid, pricing reference points.

## How To Use This Context
1. Read INTENT-CONTEXT-SYSTEM.md before doing anything else.
2. Use Layer 2 files when you need depth on a specific topic.
3. Do not guess what is not in these files. Flag the gap instead.
4. Reference DOMAIN.md for terminology before using any Restacked-specific terms.
5. Reference UX-PATTERNS.md before making any decision that affects user flows.

## If The Intent File Does Not Match Your Task
1. State the new intent clearly.
2. Read all Layer 2 files.
3. Generate a new INTENT-[PURPOSE].md following the same format as INTENT-CONTEXT-SYSTEM.md.
4. Do not start building until the new Intent file is confirmed by the user.

## Regeneration Triggers
- User requests updated context: regenerate affected Layer 2 files and the Intent file. Do not change unaffected files.
- You hit a context gap: stop, flag it explicitly, propose a new or updated file, ask the user to confirm before continuing.
- Project scope changes: regenerate INTENT-CONTEXT-SYSTEM.md and any directly affected Layer 2 files.

## Key Facts to Remember
- One MCP URL: mcp.restacked.ai
- Trigger phrase: "make it so"
- GitHub is the only persistence layer.
- No dashboard. No editor. Everything happens in the AI client.
- Vibe coders are a primary user. Plain language always. No jargon.
- V0 is the frontend build tool. No custom React outside V0 at launch.
- Prototype uses PAT. Launch uses GitHub OAuth.
